description_long: This object is a double-barreled shotgun with a classic design, featuring a long, black metal barrel and a wooden stock. The barrels are side-by-side, giving the weapon a broad, flat profile when viewed from the front or back. The stock and fore-end are made of polished wood, likely walnut, with a smooth finish and subtle grain patterns. The trigger guard and trigger are visible beneath the receiver, and the break-action hinge is subtly indicated at the joint between the barrels and the stock. The overall construction is robust, with a glossy finish on the metal parts and a matte sheen on the wood. There are no visible engravings or decorative elements, giving it a utilitarian appearance.
description: A double-barreled shotgun with a black metal barrel and polished wooden stock, featuring a classic, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the twin black barrels and the rounded end of the wooden stock.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the shotgun, highlighting the black barrels and brown wooden stock.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the end of the wooden stock and the alignment of the barrels.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the shotgun's profile and construction details.
attribute_handle_design: straight, wooden, smooth, contoured for shoulder support
